etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "easy" from Old French "aisié" + "going" (moving with ease)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Moving with ease; having relaxed flexibility and easy-going calmness with relaxed presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Moving ease → relaxed flexibility → contemporary easy-going calmness and easy-going relaxation with flexible presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Moving ease → relaxed flexibility → easy-going calmness → flexible understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Having easy-going calmness and easy-going relaxation; experiencing relaxed flexibility and easy-going ease with easy-going presence
contemporary_usage.register: Positive; indicates relaxation and flexible calmness with adaptable ease
